mappings.rationale: Directly covers the human respiratory, cardiovascular, and excretory systems, including gas exchange, circulation, and waste elimination.
Addresses the anatomical structures of respiratory organs (lungs), circulatory system (heart, blood vessels), and excretory organs (kidneys).
Directly covers blood as the fluid medium of circulation including plasma, red cells, white cells, platelets, and lymph.
Covers the diversity of respiratory, circulatory, and excretory systems across invertebrate and vertebrate phyla.
Covers plant internal transport systems including xylem and phloem, relevant to the section's treatment of internal transport in plants.
Addresses lymphocytes and lymph in vertebrates, and the role of circulating white cells in defense, relevant to the circulatory fluid components.
Provides context for gases in the environment and the respiratory challenges faced by aquatic and terrestrial animals.
Covers adaptations to aquatic gas exchange including gills and specialized respiratory structures in marine organisms.
